---
title: "Day 4 — Comparisons and Boundaries"
subtitle: "Is the value inside the limit — and is the boundary included?"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD. Generic examples (value, limit, low, high).
Core question: "Inside the limit? And is the exact edge included?"
============================================================ -->

## Comparison operators return Booleans

| Operator | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `<`  | strictly less than |
| `<=` | less than or equal (**includes** edge) |
| `>`  | strictly greater than |
| `>=` | greater than or equal (**includes** edge) |
| `==` | equal to (a **check**, not assignment) |
| `!=` | not equal to |

## Inclusive vs. exclusive — the equals bar

<!-- TODO: the central Day 4 idea. `>=`/`<=` include the exact boundary;
     `>`/`<` exclude it. -->

::: {.trap-band}
⚠️ **Watch out** — at the exact edge, `>=`/`<=` are `True` but `>`/`<` are `False`. Reversing strict vs. inclusive is the #1 boundary error.
:::

## The just-below / exact / just-above triple

<!-- TODO: clearest way to teach a boundary — show all three against one limit. -->

```{python}
#| eval: true
limit = 20.4
20.39 >= limit    # False (just below)
20.40 >= limit    # True  (exact edge included)
20.41 >= limit    # True  (above)
```

## `=` vs. `==`

<!-- TODO: `==` compares and returns True/False; `=` stores. Contrast explicitly. -->

## Type-safe comparisons

<!-- TODO: comparing a string to a number ("21.6" <= 21.6) is unsafe /
     TypeError-style — cast first with float(...). Ties back to Day 3. -->

::: {.check-band}
✅ **Non-mutating check** — a comparison like `value <= limit` on its own line just **reports** True/False; it stores nothing.
:::

## Separate evidence (sets up Day 5)

<!-- TODO: compute low_ok and high_ok SEPARATELY on Day 4;
     combining them with `and` is Day 5's job. -->

| Expression | Result | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `value >= low` | … | equality included by `>=` |
| `value > low`  | … | equality excluded by `>` |
| `value <= high`| … | equality included by `<=` |
| `value < high` | … | equality excluded by `<` |

## Show your evidence

::: {.evidence-band}
🔎 **Show your evidence** — *test* a boundary with the below/exact/above triple; *explain* each result in inclusive/exclusive language; *debug* a mixed string-vs-number comparison by naming the types.
:::
